concepts.name: freedom
concepts.definition_snippet: The power or right to speak, act, and change as one wants without hindrance or restraint

concepts.name: liberty
concepts.definition_snippet: Sometimes used interchangeably with freedom, concerns absence of arbitrary restraints

concepts.name: autonomy
concepts.definition_snippet: Giving oneself one's own laws, self-governance

concepts.name: free will
concepts.definition_snippet: The idea that entities can willingly choose an arbitrary option when presented

concepts.name: determinism
concepts.definition_snippet: The idea that every event has a cause and must have happened due to that cause

concepts.name: positive freedom
concepts.definition_snippet: The ability to fulfill one's purposes, freedom to do something

concepts.name: negative freedom
concepts.definition_snippet: Freedom from limiting forces, absence of external obstacles

concepts.name: political freedom
concepts.definition_snippet: Freedom associated with liberty and autonomy in political discourse

concepts.name: civil liberties
concepts.definition_snippet: Rights and freedoms protected from government interference

concepts.name: rights
concepts.definition_snippet: In modern liberal nations, freedom is considered a right

concepts.name: slavery
concepts.definition_snippet: Strong distinction made between freedom and slavery

concepts.name: domination
concepts.definition_snippet: Freedom from domination considered a defining aspect of freedom

concepts.name: power
concepts.definition_snippet: Operative control and reserve control needed for democracy

concepts.name: self-determination
concepts.definition_snippet: To be free is to be self-determining, autonomous

concepts.name: emergence
concepts.definition_snippet: Some propose emergence can cause free will
